title: Principal Hensley's Farewell Letter
source: A photocopied letter tacked to the corkboard in the Maple Heights school office.
pages: To the parents and students of Maple Heights Elementary — after 27 years, I am retiring. I am retiring because I have discovered that I do not assess anyone. I thought I did. I was mistaken.

The Arbiter of the Grade has been handling assessments since — as best I can determine — the year I was hired. Every report card I have signed was filled out before I opened the folder. My signature has been, technically, unnecessary.

I have not told the Arbiter that I know. It seemed rude. It seemed, more importantly, pointless. Please continue to send your children. Please continue to trust that someone is watching. Someone is. It is simply not me. Warm regards, Wendell Hensley, Principal (Retired).
